tagline: You can now copy images from your local file system and paste them into Markdown cells in Databricks notebooks using ⌘ + V (Mac) or Ctrl + V (Windows).
what_it_does: You can now copy images from your local file system and paste them into Markdown cells in Databricks notebooks using ⌘ + V (Mac) or Ctrl + V (Windows). Databricks notebooks can display images, mathematical equations, HTML, and links to other notebooks, all from Markdown cells. Display images Databricks supports displaying images in Markdown cells.
why_it_matters: Reduces operational overhead and unlocks new capabilities for data teams on Databricks.
key_takeaway: You can now copy images from your local file system and paste them into Markdown cells in Databricks notebooks using ⌘ + V (Mac) or Ctrl + V (Windows).
